news.title: English professor holds solo demonstration against Iran war
news.summary_en: Professor William J. Maxwell spoke out against the Iran war outside of Olin Library on April 7. He stood alone in front of the George Washington statue with a sign that read “No war of choice. No war on civilian targets. No war in our name. No war in God’s Name.”
news.author: Lissy Portorreal, Zoe Rhodes
news.original_title: English professor holds solo demonstration against Iran war
news.summary_cn: Professor William J. Maxwell spoke out against the Iran war outside of Olin Library on April 7. He stood alone in front of the George Washington statue with a sign that read “No war of choice. No war on civilian targets. No war in our name. No war in God’s Name.”

news.title: WashU to add Early Action and more for Class of 2031 undergraduate admissions cycle
news.summary_en: WashU Admissions is set to add an Early Action (EA) option and a priority deadline for scholarship programs for the 2026-2027 admissions cycle — adding flexibility for prospective students and streamlining the scholarship process. The University is also planning to remove its optional essay and factor demonstrated interest into its admissions decisions.
news.author: Ella Meshoulam, Victor Huang, Tanvi Gorre
news.original_title: WashU to add Early Action and more for Class of 2031 undergraduate admissions cycle

news.title: Phil Gramm draws parallels between the Industrial Revolution and the AI boom
news.summary_en: Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm argued that AI would be a force for economic good and drew parallels to the Industrial Revolution in a lecture at WashU last Thursday.
news.author: Nicholas Demba, Willa Marks
news.original_title: Phil Gramm draws parallels between the Industrial Revolution and the AI boom
